title: Aid
description: When you **help someone who has not yet rolled**

outcomes.text: The GM picks 1. Regardless, you are exposed to any risk, cost, or consequence associated with their roll
bullets: They can accomplish more than they could alone.
They gain advantage on their roll.

title: Clash (+Strength)
description: When you **fight in melee or close quarters**, roll +STR:

outcomes.text: Your maneuver works as expected (deal your damage) and pick 1:
bullets: Avoid, prevent, or counter your enemy's attack
Strike hard and fast, for 1d6 extra damage, but suffer your enemy's attack

outcomes.text: Your maneuver works, mostly (deal your damage) but you suffer your enemy's attack

title: Defend (+Constitution)
description: When you **take up a defensive stance or jump in to protect others**, roll +CON:

outcomes.text: Hold 3 Readiness (or 4 if you bear a shield).
Hold 1 Readiness (or 2 if you bear a shield).
Spend your Readiness 1-for-1 to:
bullets: Suffer an attack's damage/effects instead of your ward.
Halve an attack's effect or damage.
Draw all attention from your ward to yourself.
Strike back at an attacker (deal your damage, with disadvantage).

outcomes.text: When you go on the offense, cease to focus on defense, or the threat passes, lose any Readiness you hold.

title: Interfere
description: When you **try to foil another PC's action and neither of you back down**, roll:

outcomes.text: Roll +STR to power through or test your might; +DEX to employ speed, agility, or finesse; +CON to endure or hold steady; +INT to apply expertise or enact a clever plan; +WIS to exert willpower or rely on your senses; +CHA to charm, bluff, impress, or fit in.
They pick 1 from the list below:
They pick 1 from the list below, but you are left off balance, exposed, or otherwise vulnerable.
Options:
bullets: Do it anyway, but with disadvantage on their (next) roll.
Relent, change course, or otherwise allow their move to be foiled.

title: Know Things (+Intelligence)
description: When you **consult your accumulated knowledge**, roll +INT:

outcomes.text: The GM will tell you something interesting **and** useful about the topic at hand.
The GM will tell you something interesting—it's on you to make it useful.
Either way, the GM might ask, "How do you know this?"

title: Let Fly (+Dexterity)
description: When you **calmly take an easy shot with a ranged weapon**, deal your damage. If the shot **is tricky or you're under pressure**, roll +DEX:

outcomes.text: You have a clear shot—deal your damage.
Pick 1:
bullets: Deal your damage, but deplete your ammo (mark the next status by your weapon; don't pick this if your weapon lacks such statuses).
Hold steady and wait for a clear shot; when the moment arrives (GM's call), deal your damage.
Move to get a clear shot—exposing yourself to danger or giving up some advantage (GM says how)—then deal your damage.
Rush the shot and deal your damage, leading to a cost or complication of the GM's choice.

title: Persuade (vs. NPCs) (+Charisma)
description: When you **press or entice an NPC**, say what you want them to do (or not do). If they **have reason to resist**, roll +CHA:

outcomes.text: They either do as you want or reveal the easiest way to convince them.
They reveal something you can do to convince them, though it'll likely be costly, tricky, or distasteful.

title: Persuade (vs. PCs) (+Charisma)
description: When you **press or entice a PC and they resist**, ask their player: "Could I possibly get you to do this, yes or no?" If "No," let it drop. If "Yes," roll +CHA:

outcomes.text: They mark XP if they do what you want. If they don't, they must reveal how you could convince them.
They mark XP if they do what you want (but can refuse or make a counter-offer if they like).

title: Seek Insight (+Wisdom)
description: When you **study a situation or person, looking to the GM for insight**, roll +WIS:

outcomes.text: Ask the GM 3 questions from the list below and gain advantage on your next move that acts on the answers.
Ask 1 question from the list below and gain advantage on your next move that acts on the answer.
Questions:
bullets: What happened here?
What is about to happen?
What should I be on the lookout for?
What here is useful or valuable to me?
Who or what is really in control here?
What here is not what it appears to be?
